title: BEAI Knowledge Loop v0.1 Development Session
source_reference: Telegram direct messages #4787-#4793 and BEAI Knowledge Loop v0.1 local candidate files
user_language_aliases: 엘엘엠 위키에서 출발한 비아이 패키지 업그레이드
비아이 패키지를 상위 레벨로 끌어올리는 핵심 무기
query_examples: 자동화하면 안 된다고 한 이유가 뭐였지?
사용자의 실행 자산 정의가 어디서 나왔지?
비아이 패키지 업그레이드에서 널리지 루프가 맡는 역할은?
릴리스나 크론을 아직 하지 말라고 한 근거는?
summary: The session developed BEAI Knowledge Loop v0.1 from product direction into a manual local candidate. The loop is defined as a BEAI Package upgrade that turns AI-assisted work records and relevant external signals into reviewable user execution assets, without enabling live skill apply, cron, hooks, durable memory writes, external connectors, or release packaging.
observed_facts: BEAI Knowledge Loop v0.1 now has a local CLI prototype for distill, brief, and index flows.
The package includes source fixtures, generated dry-run outputs, companion briefs, retrieval index output, and a final check report.
The current verified state is manual v0.1 local candidate, not released.
user_decisions: BEAI Knowledge Loop should upgrade BEAI Package itself, not be framed as an owner-only or business-owner-only feature.
The core definition is the user's execution asset, not the owner's execution asset.
Actual product development should proceed through careful planning, source records, dry-runs, review, and verification before automation.
assistant_judgments: The loop is useful because it converts idea, direction correction, product judgment, boundary setting, implementation, verification, and next action into reusable knowledge.
The correct next validation step is to run one real BEAI development session through distill, brief, and index before considering automation.
Release, automation, and live memory claims must stay evidence-bound and explicitly blocked until separately approved.
inferred_patterns: A valuable BEAI development record usually contains product intent, corrected definition, implementation evidence, safety boundary, and next safe action.
The Knowledge Loop becomes more valuable when it preserves corrected wording and rejected scope instead of silently smoothing it away.
traps: Calling the candidate a released package before release packaging exists.
Turning a useful manual loop into cron, hooks, agents, or durable memory writes before repeated manual runs are proven.
Over-narrowing the product around a single user segment instead of any user developing their reality through accumulated execution assets.

knowledge_candidates.text: BEAI Knowledge Loop is a package-level knowledge and execution asset layer, not merely a wiki clone.
A real development session is a strong Knowledge Loop test case when it includes idea, correction, product definition, implementation, verification, boundaries, and next action.

execution_assets.text: Use the BEAI Knowledge Loop v0.1 development session as the next real source record for distill, companion brief, and retrieval index validation.

development_candidates.text: Add repeated manual-run evidence before promoting Knowledge Loop toward hook, cron, agent, connector, durable memory, or release packaging work.

next_actions: Generate distill JSON and markdown for this development session source record.
Generate a companion brief from the new distill output.
Regenerate the retrieval index and confirm the new record is included.
rejected_scope: Do not apply the pending live skill proposal in this step.
Do not create cron, hooks, agents, external connectors, durable memory writes, or release zip artifacts in this step.
next_safe_action: Review whether this real development-session brief is useful before promoting any automation, connector, durable memory, or release step.
